---
title: Yoneda
subtitle: <Subtitle>
date: today
filters:
  - imagify
imagify:
  header-includes: \usepackage{quiver}
---


::: {#def-natural-transformation}

A **natural transformation** between two type families $F,G:A\rightarrow\mathcal{U}$ is a family of maps
$$
    \alpha:(x:A)\rightarrow(F(x)\rightarrow G(x))
$$
We denote the type of natural transformations between $F$ and $G$ by $\mathrm{Nat}(F,G)$.

:::

::: {#thm-yoneda}

Let $y_A:A\rightarrow(A\rightarrow\mathcal{U})$ be defined by
$$
    y_A(a):\equiv \lambda x.(a=x).
$$

Then, for any type family $F:A\rightarrow\mathcal{U}$, the map

$$
    f:\mathrm{Nat}(y_A,F)\rightarrow F(A).
$$
defined by
$$

$$


:::



















pe
